title: Proven Playbook — Rankett's source-of-truth fulfillment doc + David Quaid synthesis
html:
Proven Playbook — Rankett's source-of-truth fulfillment doc + David Quaid synthesis

Source: 2026-02-28 Google Doc. The research-anchored fulfillment playbook behind Rankett's pitch — for each fulfillment line item, attach 1-3 independent studies + 1-2 expert theses + a "what we do with this" block. Yahir's defense against the "AI-search snake-oil" perception.

TL;DR

  • Core thesis of the doc: "We don't guess what works in AI search. We implement what the industry's largest datasets already proved."
  • Source-of-truth anchors named:
    • 680M+ citation analysis: brand search volume = strongest predictor of LLM citations; being on 4+ platforms 2.8x's odds of showing up in ChatGPT.
    • seoClarity AI Overview overlap study: >50% of AIO citations come from URLs already ranking in top 20.
    • Princeton-referenced GEO methods: improved AI visibility 30-40% across 10K queries.
    • Conductor + WordLift: query fan-out rewards comprehensive topic coverage.
    • Multiple analyses: AI citations align with content depth + entity clarity + technical health, NOT gimmicks.
  • Positioning language template (saveable copy):
    • "Rankett's job isn't to invent a new religion. It's to industrialize the stuff that already wins in the data—brand demand, multi-platform presence, entity clarity, and AI-friendly content depth."
  • David Quaid synthesis (extensive): schema markup is overrated for AI search; query fan-outs typically generate 3 reworded sub-prompts; Google Analytics → Reports → Generate Leads → Traffic Acquisition is the source-of-truth for tracking real-time LLM-driven traffic; reverse engineering Perplexity reveals exact ICP-language to use in content.
  • Funfluencer experiment (David Quaid via Edward Sturm E950 video): minimal content + exact intent matching beats complexity for low-competition opportunities — even in AI search.
  • Important meta-warning: "Purplexity literally just performs google searches, indexes and gives summaries..." — therefore unreliable as research source for Rankett's own content (risk of slop-citing-slop).
  • Fulfillment additions: Semantic Refresh row added (90% auto, monthly updates with fresh stats/dates, 65% freshness boost). AEO content assets +1-3/week by tier.
  • Query fan-out section: AEO/GEO subqueries for "Best carpenter in Josephine County" example.

Pattern observed

This doc is Rankett's intellectual moat. Where the build doc is operations and the 12 month plan is strategy, this is the research foundation that makes the agencies stop seeing Rankett as a vendor and start seeing it as expertise.

Two notable thinkers as Rankett's primary external authorities: David Quaid (AI Visibility & SEO Expert per the doc — practitioner) and the broader GEO research ecosystem (Princeton GEO methods, seoClarity, Conductor, WordLift). Yahir is building on top of an emerging discipline, not inventing it.

The anti-AI-content stance ("Human content outperforms every time") directly contradicts the lazy-arbitrage AI-spam playbook other agencies might pitch — it's a positioning differentiator.

The closing meta-warning (Perplexity-citing-google = risk of slop-citing-slop, "as time goes on it gets reworded, adopts new languages and over time can be completely different from others even though there is one source of truth. Think of it like the bible") is rare epistemic-humility content in the Rankett corpus.

Key quote

"This also directly means that we can manipulate google and ai to show every one of our clients as long as it's done correctly. Structure blogs as fact based data and become the answer, especially in low volume searches."

Open questions

  • David Quaid — has Yahir met him or just consumed his content?
  • The Semantic Refresh "65% freshness boost" stat — source?
  • Has Rankett actually run the David Quaid Funfluencer-style minimal-content experiments?
